_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
condition: Inflammatory Bowel Disease (Crohn's Disease, Ulcerative Colitis)
procedure_name: One Stage - Sub Total Colectomy + Ileostomy

clinical_indications: Inflammatory Bowel Disease (IBD) such as Crohn's Disease and Ulcerative Colitis when medications fail to control the symptoms or malignancies.

The major disadvantages of total colectomy with end ileostomy (retention of the rectal stump, which may be a source of residual or recurrent morbidity) are not adequately addressed.

clinical_key_pointers: One Stage Subtotal Colectomy + Ileostomy surgery is a surgical procedure recommended for patients with Inflammatory Bowel Disease (IBD) such as Crohn's Disease and Ulcerative Colitis when medications fail to control the symptoms or malignancies.

The major disadvantages of total colectomy with end ileostomy are the retention of the rectal stump, which may be a source of residual or recurrent morbidity, and the psychological, social and physical problems inherent in the stoma.
contraindications: The anastomosis should not be offered to patients with severe proctitis, poor rectal compliance, faecal incontinence, or anoperineal disease.
Preservation of the rectum is also contraindicated if dysplasia or carcinoma is present.
